title: Woodland Wins Houston
excerpt: Gary Woodland takes the Texas Children's Houston Open, Nicolai Højgaard comes in second
body:
Texas Children's Houston Open Recap

Gary Woodland has emerged victorious at the Texas Children's Houston Open, finishing with a score of -14. Nicolai Højgaard came in second at -13, while Sam Stevens tied for third with a score of -11.

Leaderboard Highlights

The leaderboard was filled with talented players, including Min Woo Lee and Michael Thorbjornsen, who both tied for fourth at -11. Sudarshan Yellamaraju and Paul Waring were among those who tied for sixth at -10.

Notable Performances

Other notable performances included Jason Day, who tied for sixth, and Tony Finau, who tied for 17th at -7. The tournament saw a mix of experienced players and newcomers, all vying for the top spot.

Stats and Trends

Woodland's Score
-14
Højgaard's Score
-13
Stevens' Score
-11

Meanwhile, the DP World Tour and LIV Golf have also been making headlines, with the Hero Indian Open and the Masters scheduled for April. LIV Golf's Singapore tournament took place earlier in March, with 4Aces GC emerging as the winning team.

Upcoming Tournaments

The golf world will be keeping a close eye on upcoming tournaments, including the Volvo China Open and the Hero Indian Open. With the Masters just around the corner, players will be looking to fine-tune their skills and make a strong showing at one of golf's most prestigious events.
